caption: vintage graphic tee + baggy jeans is the uniform i didn't know i needed. thrifted both for under $50 total and i will never recover
y2k baby tee era is back and i am not apologizing. butterfly print was sitting in someone's closet for 20 years waiting for me specifically
grunge is so back. found this flannel at a thrift store and it genuinely looks like i stole it from 1994
90s track jacket >> everything. layered mine over a mesh top and suddenly i'm main character in a coming-of-age movie
cottagecore but make it thrifted. rust cords + cream knit cardigan cost me $55 and I feel like I live in a forest
band tee as a dress with a belt is a look that has never missed and will never miss. add platform boots and that's the whole fit
dark academia aesthetic on a thrift budget is so achievable. argyle vest + straight leg trousers + chelsea boots and you look like you study at oxford
platform mary janes are THE shoe of the season and i found a pair on depop for a fraction of retail. goth-adjacent meets school girl energy
color block windbreaker found me when i needed it most. 90s athletic wear is having the moment it always deserved
low rise cargo pants are back and i've made peace with it. thrifted mine for $27 and styled them with a mesh top — full y2k moment
minimal vintage is underrated. plain navy crewneck, straight leg trousers, clean sneakers. effortless and entirely thrifted
90s slip dress over a long sleeve tee is the layered look that never goes out of style. found mine at a thrift store in perfect condition
the leather bomber jacket found at a thrift store for $75 is the investment piece i'm justifying to myself indefinitely
crochet halter top over a white tank with linen trousers — this is what i mean when i say cottagecore is a lifestyle
preppy vintage is the most slept-on thrift category. polo shirt + argyle vest + pleated trousers and you're serving ivy league on a $40 budget
denim on denim and i'm not taking questions. light wash jacket + dark wash jeans is a combo that exists outside of trends
earth tone everything. olive, rust, tan, brown. thrifting in this palette is easy because nobody wanted this stuff until now
mesh layer under the graphic tee is the move. adds texture and makes a basic tee look intentional and editorial
western accessories are trending and i refuse to pay retail for a leather belt that's identical to one at every thrift store for $12
chunky platform sneakers are back and i found white ones at poshmark in great condition. this is what secondhand shopping is for
workwear carpenter jeans are the baggiest jeans available and i will not be accepting questions. thrifted for $36 and styled with a fitted tee
velvet blazer era. emerald green velvet is the statement piece that makes every basic outfit look expensive
